steps.title: Identify the logical relationship between the sentences
steps.content:
Sentence 1 says the directors "love cats." Sentence 2 describes how they created a cat video game. The second sentence does not contradict, contrast, or qualify the first—it provides a concrete example that confirms the stated passion. The blank needs a transition that signals reinforcement or illustration.
  • Choice A — 'Intermittently,': Means "at irregular intervals," which describes timing, not the logical relationship between loving cats and making a cat game. ✗
  • Choice B — 'On the other hand,': Signals contrast or an opposing point, but the second sentence supports rather than contradicts the first. ✗
  • Choice C — 'In fact,': Introduces a statement that reinforces or gives a concrete example of what was just said—exactly the relationship here, where the cat game exemplifies their love of cats. ✓
  • Choice D — 'Nevertheless,': Signals that something happens despite an obstacle or contrary expectation, but there is no obstacle or contrast in the passage. ✗

steps.title: Confirm the transition choice
steps.content: "In fact" is the standard signal for a statement that strengthens or exemplifies the preceding claim. The directors' love of cats is exemplified by their creation of Stray—no contrast or timing relationship is involved. That matches choice C — C is correct.
